gaps: No existing test case validates that Voice Prompts & Tones settings (Calls, Hold Reminder, Incoming Call, Active Audio Tone, Answering Call, Answer/Ignore) changed on PC propagate to the Android Poly Studio Mobile app.
No test case covers the cross-platform sync direction: PC → Android. Existing test 66317771 only covers the reverse direction (Android → other devices), not PC → Android.

No test case validates the sync trigger mechanism — e.g., whether changes take effect on reconnection, power cycle, or require manual refresh in the app. The bug scenario explicitly involves waiting for sync, which is untested.

No test case covers the specific Voice Prompts & Tones sub-settings as a group. These are distinct from generic toggle/slider settings and have unique behavior (e.g., Active Audio Tone is a selection, not a toggle).

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSIONS: No test validates whether PC-side changes persist in the Android app after: (a) navigating away from Device Settings and back, (b) backgrounding/foregrounding the app, (c) killing and relaunching the app, or (d) Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY: No test accounts for device-specific behavior differences. For example, Sync 20/Sync 20+ speakerphones have Secure Connection feature that disconnects after each call, which could interfere with sync propagation. Voyager series headsets may handle settings sync differently than Sync speakerphones.

🧠 STATE TRIGGERS: No test validates exactly when the Android app reads/refreshes settings from the device. Does it poll on app foreground? On Device Settings screen entry? On Bluetooth service discovery? The bug could be a timing issue where the app shows stale cached values instead of re-reading from the device.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify PC changes propagate to Android app on reconnection
steps: 1. Connect Poly device (e.g., Sync 20, Voyager 4320) to both PC (via BT700/BT600 dongle or USB) and Android phone (via Bluetooth).
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → navigate to Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.

3. Change the following settings on PC: Set 'Calls' to 'Disabled', Set 'Hold Reminder' to 'Disabled', Set 'Incoming Call' tone to 'Ring 2', Set 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 2', Set 'Answering Call' to 'Disabled', Set 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Disabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
5. Observe all six settings values displayed in the app.
6. Disconnect Bluetooth from Android (Settings → Bluetooth → tap device → Forget, or turn off Bluetooth).
7. Reconnect Android to the Poly device via Bluetooth.
8. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
9. Observe all six settings values again.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth reconnection, all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings in the Android Poly Studio Mobile app match the values set on PC (Calls=Disabled, Hold Reminder=Disabled, Incoming Call=Ring 2, Active Audio Tone=Tone 2, Answering Call=Disabled, Answer/Ignore=Disabled). The app does not show stale cached values.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify PC changes propagate after Android app force-kill and relaunch
steps: 1. Connect Poly device (e.g., Sync 20, Voyager 4320) to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.

3. Change settings on PC: Set 'Calls' to 'Tones Only', Set 'Hold Reminder' to 'Enabled', Set 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 1', Set 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 1', Set 'Answering Call' to 'Enabled', Set 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Enabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Note the current values.
5. Press the Android Recents button and swipe Poly Studio Mobile away to force-kill the app.
6. Wait 10 seconds.
7. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
8. Observe all six settings values.
generated_testcases.expected: After force-kill and relaunch of the Android app, all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings match the values set on PC. The app reads fresh settings from the device rather than showing cached pre-change values.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify PC changes propagate after Android app background/foreground cycle
steps: 1. Connect Poly device to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
3. Set 'Calls' to 'Tones Only', 'Hold Reminder' to 'Enabled', 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 3', 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 3', 'Answering Call' to 'Enabled', 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Enabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Verify initial values.

5. On PC, change settings: Set 'Calls' to 'Disabled', 'Hold Reminder' to 'Disabled', 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 2', 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 2', 'Answering Call' to 'Disabled', 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Disabled'.
6. On Android, press the Home button to background the Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Wait 15 seconds.
8. Tap the Poly Studio Mobile icon to bring the app back to foreground.
9. Navigate to Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
10. Observe all six settings values.
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding the Android app, all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings reflect the values changed on PC in step 5. The app re-reads settings from the device on foreground, not showing stale cached values.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify PC changes propagate after navigating away and back to Device Settings
steps: 1. Connect Poly device to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
3. Set baseline values on PC: 'Calls'='Tones Only', 'Hold Reminder'='Enabled', 'Incoming Call'='Ring 1', 'Active Audio Tone'='Tone 1', 'Answering Call'='Enabled', 'Answer/Ignore'='Enabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Verify baseline.
5. On PC, change settings: 'Calls'='Disabled', 'Hold Reminder'='Disabled', 'Incoming Call'='Ring 3', 'Active Audio Tone'='Tone 3', 'Answering Call'='Disabled', 'Answer/Ignore'='Disabled'.
6. On Android, tap the back arrow to navigate from Device Settings to the main device screen.
7. Wait 5 seconds.
8. Tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
9. Observe all six settings values.
generated_testcases.expected: After navigating away from Device Settings and back, all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings in the Android app match the values changed on PC in step 5. The app refreshes settings upon re-entering the Device Settings screen.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify PC changes propagate after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle (Sync 20 with Secure Connection)
steps: 1. Connect Sync 20/Sync 20+ to both PC (via BT700/BT600 dongle) and Android phone.
2. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap Sync 20 → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Note baseline values.
3. On PC, open Poly Lens Desktop → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.

4. Change settings on PC: Set 'Calls' to 'Disabled', 'Hold Reminder' to 'Disabled', 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 2', 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 2', 'Answering Call' to 'Disabled', 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Disabled'.
5. On Android, go to Settings → Bluetooth → tap Sync 20 → select 'Forget' to disconnect.
6. Wait 10 seconds.
7. Re-pair Android phone with Sync 20 via Bluetooth.
8. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap Sync 20 → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
9. Observe all six settings values.
10. Make a mobile call from the Android phone through the Sync 20.
11. End the call. (Note: Sync 20 with Secure Connection enabled will disconnect Bluetooth after the call.)
12. Wait for Bluetooth to automatically reconnect (or manually reconnect if needed).
13. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap Sync 20 → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
14. Observe all six settings values again.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth reconnection (step 8-9), all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings match the PC-changed values. After the call ends and Secure Connection triggers a disconnect/reconnect cycle (step 12-14), the settings still match the PC-changed values and are not reset to defaults or lost.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify PC changes propagate after device power cycle
steps: 1. Connect Poly device (e.g., Voyager 4320) to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.

3. Change settings on PC: Set 'Calls' to 'Tones Only', 'Hold Reminder' to 'Enabled', 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 1', 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 1', 'Answering Call' to 'Enabled', 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Enabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Verify baseline.

5. On PC, change settings: Set 'Calls' to 'Disabled', 'Hold Reminder' to 'Disabled', 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 3', 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 3', 'Answering Call' to 'Disabled', 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Disabled'.
6. Power off the Poly device by pressing and holding the Power button for 2 seconds (or until power off).
7. Wait 10 seconds.
8. Power on the Poly device by pressing and holding the Power button for 2 seconds.
9. Wait for the device to reconnect to both PC and Android automatically.
10. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
11. Observe all six settings values.
generated_testcases.expected: After the device power cycle and reconnection, all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings in the Android app match the values changed on PC in step 5. The device persists the PC-side settings through a power cycle and the Android app reads the updated values upon reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify partial change propagation (single setting change on PC)
steps: 1. Connect Poly device to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.

3. Set all six settings to known baseline values on PC: 'Calls'='Tones Only', 'Hold Reminder'='Enabled', 'Incoming Call'='Ring 1', 'Active Audio Tone'='Tone 1', 'Answering Call'='Enabled', 'Answer/Ignore'='Enabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Verify all six match baseline.
5. On PC, change ONLY ONE setting: Set 'Incoming Call' from 'Ring 1' to 'Ring 3'. Leave all other five settings unchanged.
6. Disconnect Bluetooth from Android (Settings → Bluetooth → tap device → Forget).
7. Reconnect Android to the Poly device.
8. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
9. Observe all six settings values.
generated_testcases.expected: Only the 'Incoming Call' setting shows the PC-changed value ('Ring 3'). The other five settings ('Calls', 'Hold Reminder', 'Active Audio Tone', 'Answering Call', 'Answer/Ignore') remain at their baseline values. A single-setting change on PC does not corrupt or reset other Voice Prompts & Tones settings.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify Active Audio Tone selection syncs correctly (dropdown/selection, not toggle)
steps: 1. Connect Poly device to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
3. Set 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 1' on PC.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Verify 'Active Audio Tone' shows 'Tone 1'.
5. On PC, change 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 3'.
6. Disconnect Bluetooth from Android and reconnect.
7. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
8. Observe the 'Active Audio Tone' value.
9. On PC, change 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Off'.
10. Disconnect Bluetooth from Android and reconnect.
11. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
12. Observe the 'Active Audio Tone' value.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Active Audio Tone' setting (a selection-type setting, not a simple toggle) correctly syncs from PC to Android for all valid values: 'Tone 1', 'Tone 3', and 'Off'. The Android app displays the exact selection made on PC without truncation or fallback to default.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify sync when Android app is already open on Device Settings screen during PC change
steps: 1. Connect Poly device to both PC and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
3. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.

4. While the Android app is actively displaying the Voice Prompts & Tones screen, change settings on PC: Set 'Calls' to 'Disabled', 'Hold Reminder' to 'Disabled', 'Incoming Call' to 'Ring 2', 'Active Audio Tone' to 'Tone 2', 'Answering Call' to 'Disabled', 'Answer/Ignore' to 'Disabled'.
5. Observe the Android app screen for 30 seconds without navigating away.
6. Tap the back arrow to leave Device Settings, then tap [Device Name] → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones again.
7. Observe all six settings values.
generated_testcases.expected: The Android app may or may not update in real-time while on the screen (step 5). However, after navigating away and back (step 6-7), all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings match the values changed on PC. The app does not require a Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect to pick up PC-side changes — re-entering the settings screen is sufficient.

generated_testcases.title: [PC→Android Sync] Voice Prompts & Tones — Verify sync with Voyager series headset (device asymmetry check)
steps: 1. Connect Voyager 4320 (or other Voyager series headset) to both PC (via BT700 dongle) and Android phone.
2. Open Poly Lens Desktop on PC → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
3. Set baseline on PC: 'Calls'='Tones Only', 'Hold Reminder'='Enabled', 'Incoming Call'='Ring 1', 'Active Audio Tone'='Tone 1', 'Answering Call'='Enabled', 'Answer/Ignore'='Enabled'.
4. On Android, open Poly Studio Mobile → tap Voyager 4320 → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones. Verify baseline.
5. On PC, change settings: 'Calls'='Disabled', 'Hold Reminder'='Disabled', 'Incoming Call'='Ring 3', 'Active Audio Tone'='Tone 3', 'Answering Call'='Disabled', 'Answer/Ignore'='Disabled'.
6. Disconnect Bluetooth from Android and reconnect.
7. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap Voyager 4320 → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
8. Observe all six settings values.
9. Power cycle the Voyager headset (power off, wait 10s, power on).
10. Reconnect to Android.
11. Open Poly Studio Mobile → tap Voyager 4320 → Device Settings → Voice Prompts & Tones.
12. Observe all six settings values again.
generated_testcases.expected: The Voyager series headset behaves consistently with Sync 20: all six Voice Prompts & Tones settings changed on PC propagate to the Android app after Bluetooth reconnection (step 7-8) and persist through a device power cycle (step 11-12). No device-specific divergence in sync behavior is observed.
